name: Challah
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the 8 cups of flour; put aside.
2. Soak yeast in the water for 5 minutes.
3. Sift the flour, salt and sugar together.
4. Combine the yeast mixture with 3 cups of the flour mixture, stirring until smooth.
5. Cover the bowl with a cloth and allow to rise in warm place until double in bulk - about 30 minutes.
6. Add the beaten eggs and mix well.
7. Add the remaining flour mixture and knead for ten minutes (exactly) on a lightly floured surface until smooth.
8. Place the dough in a bowl; brush the top with melted butter and cover with cloth.
9. Allow to rise until double in bulk - about 2 hours.
10. Knead the dough for 3 minutes.
11. Divide the dough into 6 equal ropes.
12. Braid as desired - three ropes can make one challah, therefore you would have two, nice-sized challahs.
13. Let rise again for 1 hour on greased baking sheets (covered).
14. Preheat oven to 400 degrees.
15. Brush the top of each challah with melted butter and beaten egg yolk.
16. Sprinkle with poppy or sesame seeds.
17. Bake until done.
